mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates lower the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack and weak consumer demand, which suppresses corporate earnings and produces negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive momentum in real sales of final goods and housing starts confirms synchronized domestic demand expansion, driving positive quarterly equity returns through earnings growth.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized during late-cycle expansions signals capacity constraints and inflationary pressure, triggering negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth in late-cycle regimes yields positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger underlying corporate earnings fundamentals, which directly translates into positive short-term equity market returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive GDP growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, anticipated monetary tightening compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 50 percent of high-inflation triggered months over a 5-year evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Co-movement of PAYEMS up and UNRATE down fails to predict positive 20-day returns with an out-of-sample R-squared above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: During credit expansion phases, accelerating housing starts indicate credit market normalization and economic stabilization, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive HOUST momentum in credit expansion regimes yields negative 5-day returns in greater than 55 percent of cases over a 3-year window.
